extra.reasoning: The property NOT(rec_drug_a AND rec_drug_b) holds over the entire input space. rec_drug_a has exactly one rule setting it: rec_a_from_conjunction (priority 50), so rec_drug_a is true iff (asthma AND ckd_dialysis), else default false. rec_drug_b is touched by two rules: rec_b_from_trigger (priority 50, IF asthma -> true) and guard_suppress_b (priority 90, IF asthma AND ckd_dialysis -> false). Conflict resolution is per-output highest-priority-wins. The only configuration in which rec_drug_a can be true is asthma AND ckd_dialysis. In that exact configuration, both rec_drug_b rules match, and guard_suppress_b (priority 90) outranks rec_b_from_trigger (priority 50), forcing rec_drug_b := false. Therefore whenever rec_drug_a is true, rec_drug_b is suppressed to false; they can never be jointly true. The remaining inputs (prior_gi_bleed, hypertension, renal_impairment) only affect rec_drug_c, which is irrelevant to this property. The strictly higher priority of the guard rule is what guarantees mutual exclusion. File: /Users/sanjaybasu/clinical-formal-verification/experiments/runs/_judge_prompts/medication-d2-hold-128.txt
